[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:六所神社]]

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

[[Category:q--q]] [[ja:六所神社]]

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
== Sōja == * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tsuruoka)|ja|六所神社 (鶴岡市)}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka City|ja|鶴岡市}}) – {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|六所神社 (所沢市)}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Tokorozawa City|ja|所沢市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tateyama)|ja|六所神社 (館山市)}} ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市}}) – {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国}} Sōja * Rokusho-no-Miya ({{ill|Fuchu City, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}) – Currently named {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|六所神社 (市川市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}) – {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)}} ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ōiso Town|ja|大磯町}}) – {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Matsue)|ja|六所神社 (松江市)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Matsue City|ja|松江市}}) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 (豊後高田市)}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Bungotakada City|ja|豊後高田市}})
== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)
=== Chubu Region === * {{ill|Shiogama Rokusho Shrine|ja|塩釜六所神社}} ({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}, {{ill|Awashimaura Village|ja|粟島浦村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Numazu)|ja|六所神社 (沼津市)}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|六所神社 (掛川市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kakegawa City|ja|掛川市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Iwata)|ja|六社神社 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Iwata City|ja|磐田市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hamakita)|ja|六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Nishi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市西区)}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Higashi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市東区)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya City) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Toyota City|ja|豊田市}})
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|ja|六所神社 (京都市左京区)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Minamiyamashiro)|ja|六所神社 (南山城村)}} (Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Minamiyamashiro Village|ja|南山城村}})
=== Shikoku Region === * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Sanagouchi)|ja|六社神社 (佐那河内村)}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Sanagouchi Village|ja|佐那河内村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hidaka)|ja|六所神社 (日高村)}} ({{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Hidaka Village|ja|日高村}})
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|六所神社 (糸島市)}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Chikugo)|ja|六所神社 (筑後市)}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Chikugo City|ja|筑後市}})
== Notes == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} * {{ill|Kokufu Hachiman Shrine|ja|国府八幡宮}} * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}}

revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|六所神社 (糸島市)}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Chikugo)|ja|六所神社 (筑後市)}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Chikugo City|ja|筑後市}})

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

=== Chubu Region === * {{ill|Shiogama Rokusho Shrine|ja|塩釜六所神社}} ({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}, {{ill|Awashimaura Village|ja|粟島浦村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Numazu)|ja|六所神社 (沼津市)}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|六所神社 (掛川市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kakegawa City|ja|掛川市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Iwata)|ja|六社神社 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Iwata City|ja|磐田市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hamakita)|ja|六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Nishi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市西区)}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Higashi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市東区)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya City) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Toyota City|ja|豊田市}})

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
== Sōja == * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tsuruoka)|ja|六所神社 (鶴岡市)}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka City|ja|鶴岡市}}) – {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|六所神社 (所沢市)}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Tokorozawa City|ja|所沢市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tateyama)|ja|六所神社 (館山市)}} ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市}}) – {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国}} Sōja * Rokusho-no-Miya ({{ill|Fuchu City, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}) – Currently named {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|六所神社 (市川市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}) – {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)}} ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ōiso Town|ja|大磯町}}) – {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Matsue)|ja|六所神社 (松江市)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Matsue City|ja|松江市}}) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 (豊後高田市)}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Bungotakada City|ja|豊後高田市}})
== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)
=== Chubu Region === * {{ill|Shiogama Rokusho Shrine|ja|塩釜六所神社}} ({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}, {{ill|Awashimaura Village|ja|粟島浦村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Numazu)|ja|六所神社 (沼津市)}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|六所神社 (掛川市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kakegawa City|ja|掛川市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Iwata)|ja|六社神社 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Iwata City|ja|磐田市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hamakita)|ja|六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Nishi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市西区)}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Higashi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市東区)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya City) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Toyota City|ja|豊田市}})
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|ja|六所神社 (京都市左京区)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Minamiyamashiro)|ja|六所神社 (南山城村)}} (Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Minamiyamashiro Village|ja|南山城村}})
=== Shikoku Region === * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Sanagouchi)|ja|六社神社 (佐那河内村)}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Sanagouchi Village|ja|佐那河内村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hidaka)|ja|六所神社 (日高村)}} ({{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Hidaka Village|ja|日高村}})
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|六所神社 (糸島市)}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Chikugo)|ja|六所神社 (筑後市)}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Chikugo City|ja|筑後市}})

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
== Sōja == * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tsuruoka)|ja|六所神社 (鶴岡市)}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka City|ja|鶴岡市}}) – {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|六所神社 (所沢市)}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Tokorozawa City|ja|所沢市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tateyama)|ja|六所神社 (館山市)}} ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市}}) – {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国}} Sōja * Rokusho-no-Miya ({{ill|Fuchu City, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}) – Currently named {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|六所神社 (市川市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}) – {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)}} ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ōiso Town|ja|大磯町}}) – {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Matsue)|ja|六所神社 (松江市)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Matsue City|ja|松江市}}) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 (豊後高田市)}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Bungotakada City|ja|豊後高田市}})
== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)
=== Chubu Region === * {{ill|Shiogama Rokusho Shrine|ja|塩釜六所神社}} ({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}, {{ill|Awashimaura Village|ja|粟島浦村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Numazu)|ja|六所神社 (沼津市)}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|六所神社 (掛川市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kakegawa City|ja|掛川市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Iwata)|ja|六社神社 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Iwata City|ja|磐田市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hamakita)|ja|六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Nishi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市西区)}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Higashi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市東区)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya City) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Toyota City|ja|豊田市}})
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|ja|六所神社 (京都市左京区)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Minamiyamashiro)|ja|六所神社 (南山城村)}} (Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Minamiyamashiro Village|ja|南山城村}})
=== Shikoku Region === * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Sanagouchi)|ja|六社神社 (佐那河内村)}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Sanagouchi Village|ja|佐那河内村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hidaka)|ja|六所神社 (日高村)}} ({{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, {{ill|Hidaka Village|ja|日高村}})
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|六所神社 (糸島市)}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Chikugo)|ja|六所神社 (筑後市)}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Chikugo City|ja|筑後市}})

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
== Sōja == * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tsuruoka)|ja|六所神社 (鶴岡市)}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka City|ja|鶴岡市}}) – {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|六所神社 (所沢市)}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Tokorozawa City|ja|所沢市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tateyama)|ja|六所神社 (館山市)}} ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市}}) – {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国}} Sōja * Rokusho-no-Miya ({{ill|Fuchu City, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}) – Currently named {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|六所神社 (市川市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}) – {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)}} ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ōiso Town|ja|大磯町}}) – {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Matsue)|ja|六所神社 (松江市)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Matsue City|ja|松江市}}) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province }} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 (豊後高田市)}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Bungotakada City|ja|豊後高田市}})
== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)
=== Chubu Region === * {{ill|Shiogama Rokusho Shrine|ja|塩釜六所神社}} ({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}, {{ill|Awashimaura Village|ja|粟島浦村}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Numazu)|ja|六所神社 (沼津市)}} ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, {{ill|Numazu City|ja|沼津市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|六所神社 (掛川市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kakegawa City|ja|掛川市}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Iwata)|ja|六社神社 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Iwata City|ja|磐田市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Hamakita)|ja|六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu City|ja|浜松市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Nishi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市西区)}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Nagoya City|ja|名古屋市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Nagoya, Higashi-ku)|ja|六所神社 (名古屋市東区)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya City) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Toyota City|ja|豊田市}})
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Kyoto, Sakyo)|ja|六所神社 (京都市左京区)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Minamiyamashiro)|ja|六所神社 (南山城村)}} (Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Minamiyamashiro Village|ja|南山城村}})

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|六所神社 (糸島市)}} ({{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Chikugo)|ja|六所神社 (筑後市)}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Chikugo City|ja|筑後市}})

{{draft categories| [[Category:Rokusho Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrines]] [[ja:六所神社]] [[sn:okusho Shrines]]

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|12=draft}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制|12=draft}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社|12=draft}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市|12=draft}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)|12=draft}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)|12=draft}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)|12=draft}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
== Sōja == * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tsuruoka)|ja|六所神社 (鶴岡市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuruoka City|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|六所神社 (所沢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokorozawa City|ja|所沢市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Tateyama)|ja|六所神社 (館山市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tateyama City|ja|館山市|12=draft|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * Rokusho-no-Miya ({{ill|Fuchu City, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)|12=draft|12=draft}}) – Currently named {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|六所神社 (市川市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市|12=draft|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Matsue)|ja|六所神社 (松江市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue City|ja|松江市|12=draft|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 (豊後高田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bungotakada City|ja|豊後高田市|12=draft|12=draft}})
== Other Shrines == === Tohoku Region === * {{ill|Take Rokusho Shrine|ja|嶽六所神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Daisen City|ja|大仙市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Yamagata City)|ja|六社神社 (山形市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|12=draft|12=draft}}) === Kanto Region === * Former Rokusho Shrine ({{ill|Tsukuba City|ja|つくば市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}) – Abolished under the {{ill|Meiji One Village One Shrine Policy|ja|明治一村一社制|12=draft|12=draft}}, with the deity transferred to {{ill|Kaage Shrine|ja|蚕影神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Rokusho Shrine (Nakazuma Town, {{ill|Jōsō City|ja|常総市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ibaraki Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|六所神社 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusha Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|六社神社 (流山市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Nagareyama City|ja|流山市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Shisui)|ja|六所神社 (酒々井町)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Shisui Town|ja|酒々井町|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Ota)|ja|六所神社 (大田区)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Akatsutsumi)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Noge)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Setagaya, Kyūta)|ja|六所神社 (世田谷区給田)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Setagaya, Tokyo)

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines--> <!--Rokusho Shrines-->

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: 65 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:六所神社 up to rev 103656924
revision.text:
'''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）・'''六社神社'''（ろくしゃじんじゃ）は、日本の[[神社]]。この名を名乗る神社は日本全国に存在する。
社名は、六柱の神を祭神とすることによる。創建当初から六柱を祭神としていた場合、都合により六つの神社を合祀した場合などがある。
令制国の[[総社]]の中には「六所神社（六所宮）」という社名のものがいくつかある。これは、その国の[[一宮]]から六宮までの祭神を勧請して総社としたことによるものである。このことから、歴史学者[[吉田東伍]]は、「六所」とは「六か所」という意味だけではなく、管内の神社を登録・管理し統括する「録所」の意味でもあるとしている。民俗学者[[中山太郎 (民俗学者)|中山太郎]]は、「録所」は墓地の意味であるという説を唱えている。<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = 白井永二・土岐昌訓 編 |year = 1997 |title = 神社辞典 |publisher = 東京堂出版 |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
==総社== * [[出羽国]] [[六所神社 (鶴岡市上藤島)|六所神社]] （[[山形県]][[鶴岡市]]上藤島） * [[安房国]] [[六所神社 (館山市)|六所神社]] （[[千葉県]][[館山市]]正木） * [[下総国]] [[六所神社 (市川市)|六所神社]] （千葉県[[市川市]]須和田） * [[武蔵国]] 六所宮 （[[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]宮町） – [[大國魂神社]] * [[相模国]] [[六所神社 (大磯町)|六所神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷） * [[出雲国]] [[六所神社 (松江市)|六所神社]] （[[島根県]][[松江市]]大草町）
==主な六所神社== === 東北・関東地方 === * [[嶽六所神社]] （[[秋田県]][[大仙市]][[神宮寺 (大仙市)|神宮寺]]） * [[六所神社 (鶴岡市上藤島)|六所神社]] （[[山形県]][[鶴岡市]]上藤島） – [[出羽国]]総社 * [[六所神社 (常総市)|六所神社]] （[[茨城県]][[常総市]]中妻町） * [[筑波山神社#六所神社跡|六所神社]] （茨城県[[つくば市]][[臼井 (つくば市)|臼井]]） – 明治一村一社制により廃社。[[筑波山神社]]関係地。ご神体は[[蚕影神社]]に移されている。 * [[六所神社 (前橋市)|六所神社]] （[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[江木町 (前橋市)|江木町]]） * [[六所神社 (所沢市上新井)|六所神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]上新井） * [[六所神社 (市川市)|六所神社]] （[[千葉県]][[市川市]]須和田） – [[下総国]]総社 * [[六所神社 (館山市)|六所神社]] （千葉県[[館山市]]正木） – [[安房国]]総社 * [[六所神社 (酒々井町)|六所神社]] （千葉県[[印旛郡]][[酒々井町]]墨） * [[六所神社 (大田区)|六所神社]] （[[東京都]][[大田区]][[下丸子]]） * [[六所神社 (世田谷区赤堤)|六所神社]] （東京都[[世田谷区]][[赤堤]]） * [[六所神社 (世田谷区野毛)|六所神社]] （東京都世田谷区[[野毛 (世田谷区)|野毛]]） * [[六所神社 (世田谷区給田)|六所神社]] （東京都世田谷区[[給田 (世田谷区)|給田]]） * [[六所神社 (大磯町)|六所神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷） – [[相模国]]総社
===中部地方=== * [[塩釜六所神社]] （[[新潟県]][[岩船郡]][[粟島浦村]]釜谷） * [[六所神社 (沼津市)|六所神社]] （[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]岡一色） * [[六所神社 (掛川市)|六所神社]] （静岡県[[掛川市]]上垂木） * [[六所神社 (名古屋市東区)|六所神社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[東区 (名古屋市)|東区]][[矢田南]]） * [[六所神社 (名古屋市西区)|六所神社]] （愛知県名古屋市[[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[比良 (名古屋市)|比良]]） * [[六所神社 (岡崎市)|六所神社]] （愛知県[[岡崎市]][[明大寺町]]） – 松平郷の六所神社より分祀を受けたもの。 * [[六所神社 (豊田市)|六所神社]] （愛知県[[豊田市]][[坂上町 (豊田市)|坂上町]]） – 鹽竈六所明神（[[鹽竈神社]]の六所宮の神）を勧請したことによる。
===近畿地方=== * [[六所神社 (京都市山科区北花山大峰町)|六所神社]] （[[京都府]][[京都市]][[山科区]]北花山大峰町） * [[六所神社 (京都市山科区上花山旭山町)|六所神社]] （京都府京都市山科区上花山旭山町） * [[喜多六所神社]] （京都府[[福知山市]]喜多） * [[六所神社 (南山城村)|六所神社]] （京都府[[相楽郡]][[南山城村]]野殿） * [[六所神社 (南山城村北大河原)|六所神社]] （京都府相楽郡南山城村北大河原）

== 主な六所社・六所宮 ==
* 六所宮 （[[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]宮町） – [[大國魂神社]] * [[六所社 (名古屋市北区下飯田町)|六所社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[北区 (名古屋市)|北区]][[下飯田町 (名古屋市)|下飯田町]]） * [[六所社 (名古屋市北区金城町)|六所社]] （愛知県名古屋市北区[[金城 (名古屋市)|金城]]町） * [[六所社 (小牧市)|六所社]] （愛知県[[小牧市]]郷中） * 六所宮 （[[大分県]][[由布市]]湯布院町） – [[宇奈岐日女神社]]

'''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）・'''六社神社'''（ろくしゃじんじゃ）は、日本の[[神社]]。この名を名乗る神社は日本全国に存在する。
社名は、六柱の神を祭神とすることによる。創建当初から六柱を祭神としていた場合、都合により六つの神社を合祀した場合などがある。
令制国の[[総社]]の中には「六所神社（六所宮）」という社名のものがいくつかある。これは、その国の[[一宮]]から六宮までの祭神を勧請して総社としたことによるものである。このことから、歴史学者[[吉田東伍]]は、「六所」とは「六か所」という意味だけではなく、管内の神社を登録・管理し統括する「録所」の意味でもあるとしている。民俗学者[[中山太郎 (民俗学者)|中山太郎]]は、「録所」は墓地の意味であるという説を唱えている。<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = 白井永二・土岐昌訓 編 |year = 1997 |title = 神社辞典 |publisher = 東京堂出版 |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
==総社== * [[出羽国]] [[六所神社 (鶴岡市上藤島)|六所神社]] （[[山形県]][[鶴岡市]]上藤島） * [[安房国]] [[六所神社 (館山市)|六所神社]] （[[千葉県]][[館山市]]正木） * [[下総国]] [[六所神社 (市川市)|六所神社]] （千葉県[[市川市]]須和田） * [[武蔵国]] 六所宮 （[[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]宮町） – [[大國魂神社]] * [[相模国]] [[六所神社 (大磯町)|六所神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷） * [[出雲国]] [[六所神社 (松江市)|六所神社]] （[[島根県]][[松江市]]大草町）

===近畿地方=== * [[六所神社 (京都市山科区北花山大峰町)|六所神社]] （[[京都府]][[京都市]][[山科区]]北花山大峰町） * [[六所神社 (京都市山科区上花山旭山町)|六所神社]] （京都府京都市山科区上花山旭山町） * [[喜多六所神社]] （京都府[[福知山市]]喜多） * [[六所神社 (南山城村)|六所神社]] （京都府[[相楽郡]][[南山城村]]野殿） * [[六所神社 (南山城村北大河原)|六所神社]] （京都府相楽郡南山城村北大河原）
=== 中国・四国地方 ===
* [[六所神社 (松江市)|六所神社]] （[[島根県]][[松江市]]大草町） – [[出雲国]]総社 * [[六所神社 (日高村)|六所神社]] （[[高知県]][[高岡郡]][[日高村]]岩目地）
=== 九州地方 ===
* [[六所神社 (筑後市)|六所神社]] （[[福岡県]][[筑後市]]羽犬塚） – 六所宮 * [[六所神社 (糸島市)|六所神社]] （福岡県[[糸島市]]志摩馬場） * [[六所神社 (久山町)|六所神社]] （福岡県[[糟屋郡]][[新宮町]]立花口） * [[六所神社 (豊後高田市)|六所神社]] （[[大分県]][[豊後高田市]]市夷)

* 六所宮 （[[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]宮町） – [[大國魂神社]] * [[六所社 (名古屋市北区下飯田町)|六所社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[北区 (名古屋市)|北区]][[下飯田町 (名古屋市)|下飯田町]]） * [[六所社 (名古屋市北区金城町)|六所社]] （愛知県名古屋市北区[[金城 (名古屋市)|金城]]町） * [[六所社 (小牧市)|六所社]] （愛知県[[小牧市]]郷中） * 六所宮 （[[大分県]][[由布市]]湯布院町） – [[宇奈岐日女神社]]
== 主な六社神社 ==
* [[六社神社 (山形市)|六社神社]] （[[山形県]][[山形市]]見崎） * [[六社神社 (つくば市)|六社神社]] （[[茨城県]][[つくば市]]泉） * [[六社神社 (吉川市)|六社神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[吉川市]][[深井新田 (吉川市)|深井新田]]） * [[六社神社 (千葉市若葉区野呂町)|六社神社]] （[[千葉県]][[千葉市]][[若葉区]]野呂町） * [[六社神社 (千葉市若葉区古泉町)|六社神社]] （千葉県千葉市若葉区古泉町） * [[六社神社 (流山市)|六社神社]] （千葉県[[流山市]][[深井新田 (流山市)|深井新田]]） * [[六所神社 (浜松市中央区恒武町)|六社神社]] （[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[中央区 (浜松市)|中央区]]恒武町） * [[六社神社 (浜松市中央区中郡町)|六社神社]] （静岡県浜松市中央区中郡町） * [[六所神社 (浜松市中央区小沢渡町)|六社神社]] （静岡県浜松市中央区小沢渡町） * [[六社神社 (磐田市)|六社神社]] （静岡県[[磐田市]]福田） * [[六社神社 (佐那河内村)|六社神社]] （[[徳島県]][[名東郡]][[佐那河内村]]下栗見坂）

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Rokusho Shrines]]
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:同名の神社
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
