revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

City]]) – {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Bungotakada)|ja|六所神社 {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11392349}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392349]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]]
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六所神社]]
'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

'''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社, also known as '''Rokusha Shrine''' (六社神社)) is a type of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in Japan. Shrines bearing this name can be found throughout the country.
The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>

The shrine's name derives from enshrining six deities. This may have been the case from the time of its founding or due to the merging of six shrines for practical reasons.
Some of the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}} (総社, provincial shrines) in the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō period]] bear the name "Rokusho Shrine" or "Rokusho-no-Miya." These served as sōja by enshrining deities from the [[Ichinomiya]] to the Rokunomiya of the province. Historian [[Yoshida Tōgo]] argued that "Rokusho" not only means "six places" but also signifies a "registry office" for managing and overseeing shrines in the jurisdiction. Folklorist [[Nakayama Tarō]] proposed that "registry office" could imply a connection to burial grounds.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |author = Edited by Eiichi Shirai and Masanori Toki |year = 1997 |title = Jinja Jiten |publisher = Tokyodo Shuppan |isbn = 4-490-10474-X |page = p.354 }}</ref>
